summary: Verified the city grouping header restyle on Print Requests page for CEO (surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth). Headers now render with a distinct emerald-to-teal gradient (linear-gradient rgb(5,150,105) -> rgb(13,148,136)) and bold white text. Both city groups ('Mumbai' with count 2 and 'No City' with count 3) show chevron, MapPin icon, city name, and translucent-white count badge, all clearly legible. Collapse/expand toggle works: clicking Mumbai header collapsed its 2 rows (5 -> 3 visible) and clicking again restored them (3 -> 5). No layout breakage; page renders normally.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing print requests (2 in Mumbai, 3 in 'No City').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Print Requests page uses the table layout (not card/board) and renders headers via TableRow with data-testid=print-city-row-<cityName>. Gradient bg = from-emerald-600 to-teal-600. Two visible groups on test tenant: Mumbai (2), No City (3). CityGroupHeader card variant with data-testid=print-city-group-* also exists in the code but not rendered in current view (only the table variant is used on the page).
rca of the issue: N/A - CSS restyle only. Visual verification confirms emerald/teal gradient headers are now applied and clearly distinct from previous slate-100 background.
